anchor.snippet: A circle is a shape consisting of all points in a plane that are at a given distance from a given point
The distance between any point of the circle and the centre is called the radius
The length of a line segment connecting two points on the circle and passing through the centre is called the diameter
A circle bounds a region of the plane called a disc
Annulus : a ring-shaped object, the region bounded by two concentric circles
Arc : any connected part of a circle
Centre : the point equidistant from all points on the circle
Chord : a line segment whose endpoints lie on the circle
Circumference : the length of one circuit along the circle
Diameter : a line segment whose endpoints lie on the circle and that passes through the centre
Disc : the region of the plane bounded by a circle
Lens : the region common to (the intersection of) two overlapping discs
Radius : a line segment joining the centre of a circle with any single point on the circle itself
Sector : a region bounded by two radii of equal length with a common centre
Segment : a region bounded by a chord and one of the arcs connecting the chord's endpoints
Secant : an extended chord, a coplanar straight line, intersecting a circle in two points
Semicircle : one of the two possible arcs determined by the endpoints of a diameter
Tangent : a coplanar straight line that has one single point in common with a circle
A circle is a plane figure bounded by one curved line
Ferdinand von Lindemann proved that π is transcendental
The ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter is π
the area enclosed by a circle is equal to that of a triangle whose base has the length of the circle's circumference and whose height equals the circle's radius
The circle is the plane curve enclosing the maximum area for a given arc length
the radian measure 𝜃 of the angle is the ratio of the arc length to the radius
One radian is the measure of the central angle subtended by a circular arc whose length is equal to its radius
the formula for the arc length s of a circular arc of radius r and subtending a central angle of measure
the formula for the area A of a circular sector of radius r and with central angle of measure

The circle is the shape with the largest area for a given length of perimeter
Its symmetry group is the orthogonal group O(2, R )
All circles are similar
A circle circumference and radius are proportional
The area enclosed and the square of its radius are proportional
The circle that is centred at the origin with radius 1 is called the unit circle

annotations.label: Three noncollinear points determine a unique circle

anchor.snippet: Through any three points, not all on the same line, there lies a unique circle

annotations.label: Chords equidistant iff equal in length

anchor.snippet: Chords are equidistant from the centre of a circle if and only if they are equal in length

annotations.label: Perpendicular bisector of a chord passes through centre

anchor.snippet: The perpendicular bisector of a chord passes through the centre of a circle

annotations.label: Central angle is twice the inscribed angle

anchor.snippet: If a central angle and an inscribed angle of a circle are subtended by the same chord and on the same side of the chord, then the central angle is twice the inscribed angle

annotations.label: Equal inscribed angles on same side

anchor.snippet: If two angles are inscribed on the same chord and on the same side of the chord, then they are equal

annotations.label: Supplementary inscribed angles on opposite sides

anchor.snippet: If two angles are inscribed on the same chord and on opposite sides of the chord, then they are supplementary
An inscribed angle subtended by a diameter is a right angle
The diameter is the longest chord of the circle
If the intersection of any two chords divides one chord into lengths a and b and divides the other chord into lengths c and d , then ab = cd

annotations.label: Perpendicular intersecting chords sum to diameter squared

anchor.snippet: If the intersection of any two perpendicular chords divides one chord into lengths a and b

annotations.label: Sum of squared lengths of perpendicular chords

anchor.snippet: The sum of the squared lengths of any two chords intersecting at right angles at a given point is the same
The distance from a point on the circle to a given chord times the diameter of the circle equals the product of the distances from the point to the ends of the chord

annotations.label: Perpendicular to radius at endpoint is tangent

anchor.snippet: A line drawn perpendicular to a radius through the end point of the radius lying on the circle is a tangent to the circle

annotations.label: Perpendicular to tangent at contact passes through centre

anchor.snippet: A line drawn perpendicular to a tangent through the point of contact with a circle passes through the centre of the circle

annotations.label: Two tangents from external point are equal

anchor.snippet: Two tangents can always be drawn to a circle from any point outside the circle, and these tangents are equal in length

annotations.label: Tangent angles at external point are supplementary

The chord theorem states that if two chords, CD and EB , intersect at A , then AC × AD = AB × AE
If two secants, AE and AD , also cut the circle at B and C respectively
If a tangent from an external point A meets the circle at F and a secant from the external point A meets the circle at C and D respectively
The angle between a chord and the tangent at one of its endpoints is equal to one half the angle subtended at the centre of the circle

An inscribed angle (examples are the blue and green angles in the figure) is exactly half the corresponding central angle
The sagitta (also known as the versine ) is a line segment drawn perpendicular to a chord
Given the length y of a chord and the length x of the sagitta, the Pythagorean theorem can be used to calculate the radius of the unique circle that will fit around the two lines

annotations.label: Compass construction from centre and point

anchor.snippet: The simplest and most basic is the construction given the centre of the circle and a point on the circle

anchor.snippet: Construct the midpoint M of the diameter

Apollonius of Perga showed that a circle may also be defined as the set of points in a plane having a constant ratio (other than 1) of distances to two fixed foci
the circle of Apollonius for these three points is the collection of points P for which the absolute value of the cross-ratio is equal to one

anchor.snippet: If C is the midpoint of the segment AB , then the collection of points P satisfying the Apollonius condition
the locus of points P satisfying the above equation is called a "generalised circle."

anchor.section: Inscription in or circumscription about other figures
anchor.snippet: In every triangle a unique circle, called the incircle , can be inscribed such that it is tangent to each of the three sides of the triangle

anchor.section: Inscription in or circumscription about other figures
anchor.snippet: About every triangle a unique circle, called the circumcircle, can be circumscribed such that it goes through each of the triangle's three vertices

anchor.section: Inscription in or circumscription about other figures
anchor.snippet: A tangential polygon , such as a tangential quadrilateral , is any convex polygon within which a circle can be inscribed that is tangent to each side of the polygon

anchor.section: Inscription in or circumscription about other figures
anchor.snippet: A cyclic polygon is any convex polygon about which a circle can be circumscribed

anchor.section: Inscription in or circumscription about other figures
anchor.snippet: A polygon that is both cyclic and tangential is called a bicentric polygon

anchor.section: Inscription in or circumscription about other figures
anchor.snippet: A hypocycloid is a curve that is inscribed in a given circle by tracing a fixed point on a smaller circle that rolls within and tangent to the given circle

annotations.label: Circle as limit of regular polygons

anchor.snippet: The series of regular polygons with n sides has the circle as its limit as n approaches infinity
A Cartesian oval is a set of points such that a weighted sum of the distances from any of its points to two fixed points (foci) is a constant

annotations.label: Circle as ellipse with eccentricity zero

anchor.snippet: A circle is an ellipse with an eccentricity of zero
A supercircle has b = a
A Cassini oval is a set of points such that the product of the distances from any of its points to two fixed points is a constant
A curve of constant width is a figure whose width, defined as the perpendicular distance between two distinct parallel lines

annotations.label: Locus of constant sum of squared distances is a circle

anchor.snippet: The locus of points such that the sum of the squares of the distances to the given points is constant is a circle

Squaring the circle is the problem, proposed by ancient geometers , of constructing a square with the same area as a given circle
In 1882, the task was proven to be impossible, as a consequence of the Lindemann–Weierstrass theorem
Defining a circle as the set of points with a fixed distance from a point, different shapes can be considered circles under different definitions of distance
Taxicab circles are squares with sides oriented at a 45° angle to the coordinate axes

annotations.label: Unit taxicab disc is von Neumann neighborhood

anchor.snippet: A circle of radius 1 (using this distance) is the von Neumann neighborhood of its centre
A circle of radius r for the Chebyshev distance
The circle is the one-dimensional hypersphere (the 1-sphere)
In topology , a circle is not limited to the geometric concept, but to all of its homeomorphisms
An oriented circle is an ordinary circle with an orientation represented by a binary value attached to it
